title: codonanthe formicarum
intro: Codonanthe formicarum, also known as the ant plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae, native to the tropical forests of South America. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to attract and house certain species of ants, which are believed to protect the plant from herbivores. The medicinal properties of Codonanthe formicarum are attributed to its high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been shown to have antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of the plant are used to treat various health issues, including fever, skin infections, and respiratory problems, and are often taken in the form of tea or tincture.
